creator: exploringlondon
caption:
This is Chelsea in Bloom 2026, London’s largest free flower festival and here’s your full walking guide to cover the best installations this year! The theme is year is “Out of this World” and you’ll see the craziest displays, from astronauts to UFOs to rockets and aliens! Lots of the shops have specific activations to this week too, like @penhaligons so make the most of them 🌸

🚇 Start at Sloane Square Station
📍Sloane Square
📍Duke of York Square
📍King’s Road @kingsroad.london 
📍Pavilion Road @pavilionroad 
📍Sloane Street @sloanestreetsw1 
And you’ll be back at the station!

🗓️ 18th May to 24th May
🎟️ Free to see, no need to pre-book
🗺️ You can download a map from the Chelsea in Bloom website too!

transcription.text: London's free flower festival is now open until the end of this week and this video will show you the exact route to take to see all of the best installations. Start off at Sloane Square Station right outside you'll see this installation then make your way across the road to Sloane Square. This is where you'll see this massive zodiac display which is amazing and then right opposite you'll see Peter Jones which also has a beautiful display this year. From here you can walk straight on to Duke of York Square which has some beautiful displays in front of Zara and Trilogy and here you can turn right all the way down past Trinny London and you'll be on King's Road. King's Road is really long and there are so many displays to see including the massive one outside Saatchi Gallery and lots of shops also have activations specifically for Chelsea in Bloom like Penhaligon's where you get a free flower bouquet for every qualifying spend and complimentary scent profiling. Look out for the Chelsea grocer and this stunning globe installation opposite Itsu and then cross the road and walk all the way back down. There are lots of other displays to see on this side including the ones outside Whole Foods and Farm Rio. Now you've walked down King's Road make your way to Pavilion Road to see some incredible UFOs, rockets and even this massive Venus flytrap. Then turn right onto Cadogan Gardens and right again onto Sloane Street where you'll find this incredible dragon as well as this Medusa outside Oliver Peoples and then you'll be back at Sloane Square Station. This whole loop can take less than an hour and you can download a map from the Chelsea in Bloom website. Should you share this route with anyone going this week?
